---
title: "Working with Vector Spatial Data in R"
format: html
---

## Setup

## Download Data

```{r}
out_dir <- here::here('data/ak_regions')

if(!dir.exists(out_dir)) {
    knb_url <- "https://dev.nceas.ucsb.edu/knb/d1/mn/v2/object/urn%3Auuid%3Aaceaecb2-1ce0-4d41-a839-d3607d32bb58"

    download.file(url = knb_url, 
                  destfile = 'shapefile_demo_data.zip', 
                  mode = 'wb')

    unzip('shapefile_demo_data.zip', exdir = out_dir)

    file.remove('shapefile_demo_data.zip')
}
```

## Attach packages

```{r}
### Data wrangling packages:
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(here)

### Simple Features vector spatial data:
library(sf)

### Data visualization packages:
library(ggplot2)
library(leaflet)
library(scales)
library(ggspatial)
```


## Read in data

```{r}
ak_rgns_sf <- read_sf(here("data/ak_regions/ak_regions_simp.shp"))
```


## Data Exploration

## Make a quick plot

```{r}
# quick plot
plot(ak_rgns_sf)

#view is not optimized for Alaska- splits it in half

```

```{r}
#what projection/datum is being used?
st_crs(ak_rgns_sf)

#transform into Alaska Albers projection which is better suited for Alaska
ak_rgns_3338_sf <- ak_rgns_sf %>%
    st_transform(crs = 3338)

# st_crs(ak_rgns_3338_sf) ### run in console to check
#now it plots the islands in the right place
```

## Analysis

## Visualization
